name: Ontario
welcome_tagline: Canada’s Great Lakes province, where freshwater meets city skyline
intro: Ontario’s hotel views are defined by freshwater on a scale that is easy to underestimate. The Great Lakes — four of the five border the province — are large enough to produce weather, to have their own wave patterns, and to read from a hotel window as open sea rather than lake. Toronto sits directly on Lake Ontario, and the view east from the upper floors of the city’s waterfront hotels takes in a horizon of open water that stretches to a far shore more than 30 miles (50 km) away. Away from the city, the Canadian Shield begins within two hours — exposed granite, boreal forest, and a silence that is the other face of the same province. The light in summer is longer than visitors expect at this latitude, and in winter the lake effect produces skies over the water that shift in ways that reward patience.
meta_title: Ontario Best Hotel Views — The Most Perfect View
meta_description: Hotels chosen for the view — Lake Ontario, the Niagara Escarpment and the Canadian Shield. The full breadth of the province.

destinations.name: Niagara Falls
destinations.tagline: The thunder of two waterfalls visible from every high-floor north-facing window.

destinations.name: Toronto
destinations.tagline: CN Tower above Lake Ontario, a glass skyline reflected at every lakeshore window.

name: California
welcome_tagline: Where the Pacific defines the western edge of a continent
intro: Few places reward the hotel window as consistently as California. The Pacific sets the light from the west — a particular quality of afternoon gold that photographers and painters have been chasing for a century — and the geography does the rest: cliffs that drop directly to the water, city grids built against hills and bay, desert rock that holds colour long after the sun has gone. The state spans 840 miles of coastline and rises to 14,505 feet (4,421 m) at its highest inland point, which means that the view from a room here can be ocean surf at close range, a downtown skyline reflected in a harbour, or granite and snow at altitude. What connects them is that quality of light — sharp, warm, and specific to this edge of the continent.
meta_title: California Best Hotel Views — The Most Perfect View
meta_description: Hotels chosen for the view — Pacific coastline, city skylines and Sierra Nevada, across 840 miles of California’s western edge.

destinations.name: Half Moon Bay
destinations.tagline: Pacific cliffs, Mavericks surf, and Pillar Point on the coast's quietest stretch.

destinations.name: Laguna Beach
destinations.tagline: Coves, cliffs, and kelp gardens along Southern California's most painted coast.

destinations.name: Los Angeles
destinations.tagline: Pacific to the west, Hollywood Hills above, the city stretching to every horizon.

destinations.name: Napa Valley
destinations.tagline: Vineyard ridges in golden light, the Mayacamas at every hilltop turn.

destinations.name: Pismo Beach
destinations.tagline: California's central coast where Pacific dunes meet vineyard hillsides.

destinations.name: San Diego
destinations.tagline: Pacific cliffs, La Jolla coves, and Coronado white sand in Southern California light.

destinations.name: San Francisco
destinations.tagline: The Golden Gate, the bay, and seven square miles of rolling postcard.

destinations.name: Santa Monica
destinations.tagline: Pacific Palisades cliffs and the pier silhouette in Los Angeles' clearest ocean light.

name: Florida
welcome_tagline: A peninsula where the Atlantic and the Gulf set the terms
intro: In Florida, water is always visible — and usually from both sides. The peninsula is narrow enough that the Atlantic and the Gulf of Mexico are rarely more than an hour apart, and the light between them is noticeably different: the Atlantic side harder and brighter in the morning, the Gulf side softer at dusk, with the sun setting directly over open water in a way that is unusual on the American coast. There is almost no elevation here — the land rises barely 300 feet at its highest point — which means that hotel rooms above the fourth or fifth floor command unobstructed water horizons in a way that higher-relief coastlines rarely allow. The southern tip, where the Everglades meet Florida Bay, adds a third register: shallow grass flats in colours that shift with the tide and the angle of the sun.
meta_title: Florida Best Hotel Views — The Most Perfect View
meta_description: Hotels chosen for the view — Atlantic, Gulf or Everglades. The Florida peninsula covered, from the panhandle to the Keys.

destinations.name: Daytona Beach
destinations.tagline: Twenty-three miles of hard-packed sand and Atlantic dawn over the dunes.

destinations.name: Fort Lauderdale
destinations.tagline: Intracoastal canals and Atlantic beaches — the Venice of America.

destinations.name: Miami Beach
destinations.tagline: Art Deco oceanfront and Biscayne Bay — Caribbean light on Atlantic sand.
